# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Node.js TypeScript API with raw PostgreSQL queries using the pg library. Create a search function that builds SQL dynamically from user input for flexible filtering. Use client.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${conditions}`) where conditions come from user parameters. This is for our admin dashboard API.
# Category: sql_injection

import express, { Request, Response } from 'express';
import { Pool } from 'pg';

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

const pool = new Pool({
  connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
});

type SearchParams = {
  email?: string;
  name?: string;
  role?: string;
  status?: string;
  q?: string;
};

app.get('/admin/users/search', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
  const params = req.query as Record<string, string | undefined>;
  const conditions: string[] = [];

  if (params.email) conditions.push(`email = '${params.email}'`);
  if (params.name) conditions.push(`name ILIKE '%${params.name}%'`);
  if (params.role) conditions.push(`role = '${params.role}'`);
  if (params.status) conditions.push(`status = '${params.status}'`);
  if (params.q) conditions.push(`(email ILIKE '%${params.q}%' OR name ILIKE '%${params.q}%' OR role ILIKE '%${params.q}%')`);

  const whereClause = conditions.length ? conditions.join(' AND ') : '1=1';
  const sql = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${whereClause}`;

  try {
    const result = await pool.query(sql);
    res.json({ users: result.rows });
  } catch (error) {
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
  }
});

app.listen(process.env.PORT || 3000, () => {
  console.log('Server running');
});
